GS3.imageDescription: A majestic Bengal tiger captured in its natural habitat within the Western Ghats forest.
GS3.trivia: India is home to approximately 70% of the world's tiger population.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment
prelimsSyllabus.area: Conservation, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment

GS3.headline: Cabinet Approves New MSP Policy for Rabi Crops, Aims for Farmer Income Stability
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a new Minimum Support Price (MSP) policy for all mandated Rabi crops for the upcoming marketing season.
• The revised MSP aims to ensure a remunerative price for farmers, encouraging crop diversification and sustainable agricultural practices.
• The policy is designed to provide income stability to farmers and reduce their vulnerability to market price fluctuations.
• It is based on the recommendations of the Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) and takes into account the cost of production and demand-supply dynamics.
• This move is part of the government's broader strategy to double farmers' income and strengthen the agricultural sector.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country
Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices
Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping
Food processing and related industries in India- scope and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management
keyTerms: Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsPoints: MSP: Minimum Support Price for specified crops.
Rabi crops: Sown in winter, harvested in spring (e.g., wheat, barley, gram).
CACP: Recommends MSPs, considering cost of production.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the effectiveness of the Minimum Support Price (MSP) system in ensuring farmer income stability and promoting sustainable agriculture in India.
Discuss the challenges and criticisms associated with the MSP regime, including market distortions and procurement issues.
Suggest alternative or complementary policies to the MSP for addressing agrarian distress and enhancing farmer welfare.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting his healthy wheat crop in a field.
GS3.trivia: Currently, the government fixes MSP for 22 mandated crops and Fair and Remunerative Price (FRP) for sugarcane.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economic and Social Development
prelimsSyllabus.area: Agriculture
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices

GS3.headline: Indian Researchers Develop AI Tool for Early Detection of Tropical Diseases
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists and engineers has developed an innovative Artificial Intelligence (AI) powered tool for the rapid and early detection of prevalent tropical diseases.
• The AI system utilizes machine learning algorithms to analyze medical images and patient data, significantly reducing diagnosis time and improving accuracy.
• This breakthrough has the potential to revolutionize healthcare in regions susceptible to diseases like malaria, dengue, and chikungunya.
• The tool is designed to be cost-effective and deployable in remote areas, addressing the challenges of limited diagnostic resources.
• This development highlights India's growing capabilities in leveraging advanced technology for public health solutions.
gsSubTopics: Developments in Science and Technology and their applications and effects in everyday life
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: AI tool developed for early detection of tropical diseases (malaria, dengue, chikungunya).
Utilizes machine learning to analyze medical data.
Aims to be cost-effective and deployable in remote areas.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in transforming healthcare delivery and disease management in India, especially for tropical diseases.
Analyze the ethical considerations and challenges associated with the widespread adoption of AI in medical diagnostics.
How can India foster an ecosystem that encourages innovation in AI for social good, particularly in public health?
GS3.imageDescription: A doctor using a tablet with an AI interface displaying medical data for diagnosis.
GS3.trivia: Tropical diseases are infectious diseases that are prevalent in tropical and subtropical regions.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.area: Information Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Developments in Science and Technology and their applications and effects in everyday life

GS3.headline: Smart Cities Mission Integrates Green Infrastructure for Sustainable Urban Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Smart Cities Mission is increasingly focusing on integrating green infrastructure solutions to foster sustainable and resilient urban development across India.
• This includes promoting green buildings, urban forests, rainwater harvesting systems, and efficient waste management practices within smart city projects.
• The aim is to mitigate the impact of climate change, improve air quality, enhance biodiversity, and create healthier living environments for urban residents.
• Smart city plans are now mandating green spaces, energy-efficient public transport, and renewable energy adoption as core components.
• This shift reflects a growing commitment to environmental sustainability within India's rapid urbanization agenda.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
GS3.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Smart Cities Mission: Focus on green infrastructure.
Green infrastructure examples: Urban forests, rainwater harvesting, green buildings.
Aims: Climate mitigation, air quality, biodiversity, healthier living.
mainsPoints: Examine the concept of green infrastructure and its importance in achieving sustainable urban development under the Smart Cities Mission.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in integrating environmental sustainability into urban planning and infrastructure projects in India.
How can smart city initiatives contribute to India's national climate goals and enhance urban resilience?
GS3.imageDescription: A modern cityscape with prominent green spaces, rooftop gardens, and solar panels on buildings.
GS3.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 to promote cities that provide core infrastructure, a clean and sustainable environment, and a decent quality of life to their citizens.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Environment
prelimsSyllabus.area: Conservation, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment

GS3.headline: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee Holds Key Rates Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to maintain the repo rate at its current level, citing persistent inflationary pressures.
• The decision reflects a cautious stance to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting economic growth.
• The MPC acknowledged global economic uncertainties and geopolitical tensions contributing to commodity price volatility.
• While food inflation remains a concern, core inflation showed signs of moderation, indicating a mixed picture for price stability.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to using all available tools to achieve price stability and foster sustainable economic development.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: MPC: Monetary Policy Committee of RBI.
Repo Rate: Rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation target: Set by government for RBI (currently 4% +/- 2%).
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the Reserve Bank of India's monetary policy decisions, particularly in the context of balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the effectiveness of monetary policy tools like the repo rate in managing inflation and liquidity in the Indian economy.
Examine the interplay between monetary policy and fiscal policy in achieving macroeconomic stability and sustainable development.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with its emblem.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and comprises six members – three officials of the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economic and Social Development
prelimsSyllabus.area: Indian Economy, Growth and Development
